title: How To Shine As Biotech Comes Back From the Brink
snippet: Biotech has been pulling off some remarkable science of late, while M&A and IPOs return to bolster the sector. But that doesn’t mean the industry is back from the brink. BioSpace gathered three early-stage biotech CEOs and members of the NextGen Class of 2026 to talk about surviving and thriving in this rollercoaster...

title: Update: Reversal after 20 deaths allows new Japan patients to take Amgen's rare disease drug Tavneos
snippet: Kissei Pharmaceutical is reversing a recommendation related to Amgen-shared Tavneos that it made just a few days ago, now saying the rare disease drug can be given to new patients.

title: Roche licensing pact clears generic drugmakers to manufacture antiviral flu drug
snippet: By partnering with a UN-backed body, Roche has enabled companies to make the medicine for supply in 129 countries.

title: Lilly’s triple agonist shows bariatric surgery–like weight loss results
snippet: Lilly met analysts’ sky-high expectations with 28.3% weight loss over 80 weeks for the triple hormone receptor agonist retatrutide in a highly anticipated readout on Thursday.

title: Vaxart seeks shareholder support as battle over stock split brews
snippet: In September last year, a group of concerned stockholders raised alarm about Vaxart’s proposed reverse stock split, which the biotech was pushing for despite strong opposition from shareholders.

title: Lilly, BMS, Incyte strike deals to keep biopharma’s AI integration rolling
snippet: A trifecta of newly inked tech partnerships—from Eli Lilly, Bristol Myers Squibb and Incyte—exemplify the increasingly central role that AI is playing in drug development.

title: BioMarin bounces back as Voxzogo performs ‘bigger than expected’ in new indication
snippet: After being hit by safety issues and subpar results in another trial, BioMarin’s Phase 3 test of Voxzogo for a rare skeletal disorder called hypochondroplasia showed efficacy “solidly above” what the drug has shown for achondroplasia, which causes dwarfism.

title: Inside Germany’s biotech capital infrastructure and innovation engine
snippet: In this episode of Denatured, as part of our series on the European life sciences investment ecosystem, you'll be hearing from Regina Hodits, managing director at Angelini Ventures and Sofia Ioannidou, VC partner at Andera Life Sciences. They explore Germany’s biotech and life sciences ecosystem, including the science...

title: Biopharma professionals increasingly accept underemployment
snippet: The number of biotech and pharma professionals taking jobs they’re overqualified for is now over 50%, based on a BioSpace LinkedIn poll. A recruiting firm executive discusses the trend, the reasons behind it and why it doesn’t have to derail careers.

title: Single-cell and spatial transcriptomics reveal SPRR2Aepithelial-MMP1fibroblast interactions as key drivers of Esophageal Squamous Cell Carcinoma progression.
snippet: Esophageal squamous-cell carcinoma is a highly prevalent and lethal malignancy, and its tumor ecosystem is complex and poorly understood; therefore, how malignant epithelial states cooperate with stromal fibroblasts to drive tumor progression remains unclear. Here, we constructed a comprehensive single-cell atlas of primary ESCC tumors, which enabled the su...

title: MIF mediates monocyte-dependent inhibition of natural killer cell cytotoxicity in triple-negative breast cancer.
snippet: Immunotherapies remain ineffective in triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC), underscoring the need to define drivers of immune suppression. Natural killer (NK) cells are crucial for eliminating disseminated tumor cells (DTCs) through NK cell cytotoxicity (NKCC), but this is impaired in metastasis. While tumor cell-intrinsic mechanisms of NK cell evasion are....

title: Quantitative coupling of clonal CNV evolution and spatially restricted malignant states identifies MFGE8 as a candidate late-state-associated target in breast cancer.
snippet: Breast cancer progression reflects heterogeneous malignant states shaped by clonal evolution and microenvironmental selection, yet how genomic instability quantitatively couples to transcriptional state transitions and spatial niche organization remains unclear. We integrated single-cell RNA sequencing and spatial transcriptomics across ER-positive, HER2-po...

title: Transcriptome graph transformer: a graph transformer-based unsupervised model for transcriptome data analysis.
snippet: Rapidly growing transcriptomic datasets pose challenges for traditional analytical methods, which struggle with high dimensionality, heterogeneity, and nonlinear gene relationships. Existing deep learning models often require fixed-length inputs and fail to integrate biological network information. We introduce Transcriptome Graph Transformer (TGT), an unsu...

title: Identification of Cervical Cancer Biomarkers Using Gene Co-Expression Networks and Machine Learning Methods.
publicationName: Studies in health technology and informatics
journalName: Studies in health technology and informatics
snippet: Cervical cancer (CC) causes significant mortality due to late diagnosis and limited understanding of its molecular drivers. The complex gene co-expression patterns associated with CC remain poorly characterized. Identifying key genes that distinguish tumors from normal tissue can significantly improve early diagnosis and treatment strategies. This study com...

title: Comparing Weight Loss Prior to Pancreatic Cancer Diagnosis in Cases and Controls: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
publicationName: Studies in health technology and informatics
journalName: Studies in health technology and informatics
snippet: Pancreatic cancer, characterised by high mortality and late diagnosis, urgently requires improved early detection methods. Unintentional weight loss, reported by 70-75% of patients prior to diagnosis, may serve as a key early marker. This systematic review and meta-analysis evaluated the extent of pre-diagnostic weight loss in pancreatic cancer patients com...
